definitions: To explain something to someone, typically a woman, in a condescending or patronizing way that assumes the explainer knows more about the subject than the listener, often regardless of the listener's actual knowledge or expertise.
To explain something in a way that is unnecessarily detailed, oversimplified, or self-important, especially when the explanation is given by a man to a woman.
examples: He started to mansplain the concept to her, even though she was an expert in the field.
I wish he wouldn't mansplain every technical detail.
She felt he was mansplaining when he broke down the basics of her own job.
